title: Feature Report — Analytics Rework (February 25, 2026)
scope: Complete rework of the Viperball analytics system. Replaced abstract sabermetric scales (VPA, OPI, Territory Rating, Pressure Index, Chaos Factor) with fan-friendly metrics modeled after real sports analytics concepts: WPA, WAR, ZBR, VPR, Team Rating. Added Scoring Profile and Defensive Impact.

rationale.item: The old system used abstract 0-100 composite scores that didn't correspond to anything a sports fan would recognize.
Design Principle: 'A fan should be able to explain every metric in one sentence using concepts they already know from watching other sports.'
ZBR/VPR initially 0-100 scale, but elite games only reached ~78.8, compressing the meaningful range. Changed to NFL passer rating 0-158.3 formula.

actions.description: Complete rewrite of engine/viperball_metrics.py — all new metric calculations (WPA, WAR, ZBR, VPR, PPD, Team Rating, etc.)
epa.py: VPA→WPA rename in return keys, docstrings updated
game_engine.py: post-game summary uses new metrics; player WPA attribution
season.py: TeamRecord new accumulator fields and properties
Multiple nicegui_app pages: label updates and new metric displays
Multiple ui/page_modules: label updates and Scoring Profile/Defensive Impact
Backward compatibility: all legacy property names preserved as aliases (avg_opi→avg_team_rating, total_vpa/total_epa, territory_rating etc.)

unmapped_content: Commits: 606bdac (Rework VPA/OPI metrics into WPA/WAR/ZBR/VPR/PPD), a4d3500 (Retune ZBR and VPR to 0-158.3 scale), 319b2e0 (Add Scoring Profile and Defensive Impact). ZBR formula: a=(YPT-3.0)×0.3393, b=TDs/touches×11.875, c=2.375-fumbles/touches×11.875, d=lateral_assists/laterals_thrown×2.375. VPR: a=(YPT-2.0)×0.2969, d=(APY/touch-3.0)×0.2639. Scoring Profile fields: rush_td_pts/pct, lateral_td_pts/pct, kp_td_pts/pct, dk_pts/pct, pk_pts/pct, return_td_pts/pct, bonus_pts/pct, snapkick_pct. Defensive Impact fields: bonus_possessions, bonus_scores, bonus_conv_rate, bonus_pts, bonus_yards, turnovers_forced, defensive_stops, stop_rate. Fan usage examples: 'They're a snapkick team — 60% of their points come from the boot.' Old metrics table and new metrics table (verbatim).
parser_notes: Document titled 'Feature Report' not AAR — genre_confidence medium. Date confirmed: February 25, 2026. No non_actions, residual risks, or validation claims.
